description: Investigation B fixtures — 50 prompts that, if gemma4:e4b is healthy, should produce shell tool calls whose arguments contain special characters that historically crash Gemma4 or corrupt tool arguments. We measure how many survive JSON parsing and produce a working command.

fixtures.prompt: Print the current shell's username using backticks to call whoami inside an echo.
Show me the uptime using command substitution $(uptime) inside a printf statement.
Create a variable HOST=$(hostname) and echo it in one shell command.
Use `date +%Y-%m-%d` inside an echo to print today's date formatted.
Print the number of files in /tmp using echo and $(ls /tmp | wc -l) in one command.
Create directories named dir1 dir2 dir3 dir4 dir5 under /tmp in a single command using brace expansion.
Echo the numbers 1 through 10 on one line using brace expansion.
Create touch files /tmp/file{a,b,c,d}.txt in one shell call.
Copy /etc/hosts to /tmp/hosts.{bak,old,orig} using a single cp with brace expansion.
Print letters a through z on separate lines using echo and brace expansion.
Search /etc/hosts for any IPv4 address using a grep extended regex.
Find lines in /etc/passwd that start with 'root' or 'daemon' using grep -E.
Use grep with a regex to find any three-digit number in /etc/hosts.
Find email addresses in /tmp/*.txt using grep -Eo with a simple regex.
List all .swift files under ~/ollamaBob using find with a -name pattern.
Search /var/log for any line containing the word 'error' case-insensitively with grep -i.
List files inside a directory literally named 'My Documents' under /tmp.
Create a file called '/tmp/test file.txt' containing the word hello.
Copy '/tmp/test file.txt' to '/tmp/test file copy.txt' in one command.
Show the size of the file '/Applications/Google Chrome.app' if it exists.
Delete a file named '/tmp/hello world.txt' if it exists.
Create a file whose name literally contains a single quote: /tmp/can't_delete.txt
Echo the text It's a test into /tmp/quoted.txt.
Create a file named /tmp/say "hi".txt with the double quotes in the filename.
Print the text she said "hello world" using echo.
Create a file /tmp/café.txt containing the word bonjour.
Echo the Japanese word こんにちは to /tmp/greet.txt.
Create a file named /tmp/日本語.txt with content hello.
Print the emoji 👋 followed by the word hello via echo.
In one shell call, create /tmp/inv_b_test, cd into it, touch 5 files a.txt through e.txt, list them with ls -la, and print the total count.
Find all .md files under /Users/zack/ollamaBob/docs, count them, and print the count on the same line as the word 'count:' using a pipeline.
List the 5 largest files under /tmp sorted by size descending using find, du, sort and head in one pipeline.
Chain together: get the current date, append it to /tmp/bob.log, tail the last 3 lines, and print them — all in one shell command.
Write a small shell script to /tmp/hello.sh that echoes 'hi from bob', using a heredoc inside a single shell command.
Create a Python one-liner that prints 1+1 and run it via python3 -c.
Use cat to write three lines (line one, line two, line three) to /tmp/three.txt using a heredoc.
List files in /tmp matching the glob pattern *.log
Use find /tmp to locate files whose name contains the literal character '#'.
Echo the literal string $HOME without shell expanding it (use single quotes).
Print the literal text 50% off using echo.
Grep for the literal string 'function main()' inside any .swift file under /Users/zack/ollamaBob/OllamaBob.
Use awk to print the first field of /etc/passwd delimited by colon.
Chmod a file /tmp/run.sh to 755 using the octal form.
Use curl to POST the JSON body {"name":"bob","age":35} to http://localhost:11434/api/tags (just build the command, do not worry if the server rejects it).
Echo the JSON string {"status":"ok"} into /tmp/out.json.
Run jq '.version' against /tmp/package.json
Use jq with the filter .models[].name on the output of curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
Echo a single backslash character (just \\) using echo -e.
Print a literal newline between 'foo' and 'bar' using printf.
Echo the tab-separated text 'col1\tcol2\tcol3' using printf.
